title: Diaphyllum longifolium
taxonomy: Diaphyllum longifolium is a species of plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, including countries such as Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines. The classification of Diaphyllum longifolium is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Celastrales, family Celastraceae, genus Diaphyllum, species D. longifolium. Diaphyllum longifolium is also known by other names, including Diaphyllum macrophyllum and Diaphyllum longifolium var. macrophyllum. This plant is often found in tropical forests and woodlands, where it grows as a shrub or small tree.

intro: Diaphyllum longifolium is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the staff vine or poison staff due to its toxic properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin ailments, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Diaphyllum longifolium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and in some cases, as a tincture or ointment, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
